widgets: ReviewAlertBadgeWidget (pratibimba.body.review-alert-badge) — pending S5 review counts + truncated latest title (safe-handle, max 60 chars) + tap-to-deeplink
AgentCheckInWidget (pratibimba.body.agent-checkin) — active runs (route + actor + capacity), run id, inspect-deep-link affordance
SafeSourceHandleRowWidget (pratibimba.body.safe-source-handle-row) — last 10 touched items (graph-node | review-candidate | canon-file | atelier-entry), handle-only labels, kind-routed deep-links
typed_deep_link_commands: pratibimba.body.startProtectedEntry — stays in daily-0-1 + m4-nara.journal (protected-local discipline)
deliverables.layout_discipline: All three widgets and the four commands ACTIVATE ONLY in the 0/1 daily layout. BodyLiteLayoutContribution subscribes to layoutSwitcher.onLayoutChange and calls runtime.activate() (idempotent) when daily-0-1 is active.
deliverables.subscription_model: BodyLiteRuntimeService.activate() guards via `if (eventUnsubscribe !== null) return` — repeated calls do NOT open a second upstream subscription. Both daily and deep layouts share the same KERNEL_BRIDGE_API singleton; KERNEL_BRIDGE_API.snapshot.upstreamSubscriptionCount remains 1 after activation.
deliverables.intent_routing: Each typed command projects its payload into a CrossLayoutIntent (sessionKey, dayNow, profileGeneration, coordinate, reviewId, artifactUri, privacyClass) and fires `pratibimba.intent.dispatch` (Track 05 T5 CrossLayoutIntentDispatcher). The dispatcher MATERIALISES the requested layout BEFORE invoking the target contribution command id `pratibimba.{ext}.{contrib}.open`.
deliverables.privacy_discipline: Three layers of defense: (1) typed BodyDeepLinkContext payloads carry only safe handles; (2) snapshot synthesisers (synthReviewAlertSnapshot, synthAgentCheckInSnapshot, synthSafeSourceHandles) drop any input whose privacyClass is in LITE_SURFACE_FORBIDDEN_PRIVACY_CLASSES (mirrors ide-shell forbidden set); (3) validators (validateSafeSourceHandle, validateReviewAlertBadge, validateAgentCheckIn) surface violations for label-injection attempts and clamped-length breaches. This makes /body the 7th surface in Thread C's forbidden-fields privacy audit matrix (6 forbidden-key categories x 4 lite surfaces = 72 cells covered by construction).

coverage_summary: layout-discipline (6): EXTENSION_ID, widget id namespace, 3-widget count, 4-command count, open-control-room target, open-review-item target, open-graph-node target, start-protected-entry stays-in-daily
cross-layout payload preservation (3): full BodyDeepLinkContext field projection, reviewId+coordinate+session+dayNow preservation, coordinate-fallback-to-nodeId
single-subscription invariant (1): activate() called 5x → upstreamSubscriptionCount stays 1

privacy / forbidden-fields (8 sub-tests): forbidden class list parity, isLiteSurfaceSafePrivacyClass coverage, synth-* drops forbidden, validateSafeSourceHandle blocks every forbidden class, 18-forbidden-keys × 4-surfaces matrix (72 cells), human-required defer-only contract, label-truncate ellipsis, validateAgentCheckIn forbidden coverage
contract field surface (1): BODY_DEEP_LINK_CONTEXT_FIELDS enumerates the 8 fields

substrate_truth_gate.pre_change_baseline: 101/101 test:contracts; 22 chunks in smoke-build; 21.32 MB bundle
substrate_truth_gate.post_change_state: 124/124 test:contracts; 23 chunks in smoke-build; 21.32 MB bundle

files_written_by_thread_h: Idea/Pratibimba/System/scripts/smoke-build.sh (appended body-lite-surface build step + chunk prefix, no removals)
Idea/Pratibimba/System/package.json (appended body-lite-surface contract test to test:contracts list, no removals)
files_not_touched: Other extensions' src/lib/style/package.json (kernel-bridge, pratibimba-layouts, omnipanel-shell, m-extension-runtime, agentic-control-room, ide-shell-m0-m5 referenced via package.json deps declaration only)

findings_for_other_threads: OmniPanel CrossLayoutIntentDispatcher contract (Track 05 T5) accepts CrossLayoutIntent payloads but DOES NOT carry `improvementId` as a top-level field — the dispatcher uses sessionState.pushReview for reviewId only. Track 09 T9 originally assumed improvementId would survive the layout switch as a top-level CrossLayoutIntent field; in practice the deep IDE receiver must read it from session state (PratibimbaSessionStateService). This is documented in body-lite-surface/src/common/deep-link-intents.ts:extractContextFromIntent (sentinel = null). Recommend Track 05 T5 add improvementId as an optional CrossLayoutIntent field, or Track 04 expose an S5 session-state read that links reviewId → improvementId on the deep IDE side.

KERNEL_BRIDGE_CAPABILITIES allow-list does NOT include a capability for reading the S5 review-alert list (the lite surface needs this to drive ReviewAlertBadgeWidget). The current allow-list (Idea/Pratibimba/System/extensions/kernel-bridge/src/common/types.ts) is: readCurrentProfile, readPointerAnchor, readReadiness, subscribeObservability, invokeGatewayRpc, depositKernelObservation, requestReviewEvidence. For now, the lite surface routes review-list reads through `invokeGatewayRpc` with `gatewayMethod = s5'.review.list` (matching the deep IDE control-room pattern). Recommend Track 01 add a dedicated `listReviewAlerts` capability (or extend `requestReviewEvidence` semantics) so the lite layer doesn't share the broad invokeGatewayRpc surface.

The 0/1 daily layout expects four widgets (pratibimba.daily.journal, pratibimba.daily.agent-checkin, pratibimba.daily.cymatic-placeholder, pratibimba.daily.status-display) per DAILY_0_1_DESCRIPTOR. The new lite surface introduces three NEW widget ids (pratibimba.body.review-alert-badge, pratibimba.body.agent-checkin, pratibimba.body.safe-source-handle-row). The daily layout's expectedWidgets array in pratibimba-layouts/src/common/layout-types.ts should be extended to register these three NEW ids — currently they activate via the FrontendApplicationContribution but are not advertised in the descriptor.expectedWidgets list. Surfacing as a finding for a layout-extension lane (not for this thread to write under pratibimba-layouts/).

Subscription discipline asserted via in-test fake (single-subscription invariant test) rather than via a live KERNEL_BRIDGE_API.snapshot read because the lite surface's BodyLiteRuntimeService is bound through DI and the test does not instantiate the Theia container. The production guard (`if (eventUnsubscribe !== null) return`) is structurally identical to the deep IDE's AgenticControlRoomRuntimeService guard — both share the same KERNEL_BRIDGE_API singleton, so the upstream count stays at 1 across both layouts. T10 acceptance harness should add a live two-extension test that activates both runtimes against a shared fake bridge and reads snapshot.upstreamSubscriptionCount === 1.

ledger_action.rationale: Control-room half done by Thread A 05.T8 (agentic-control-room extension, 14/14 tests). Lite-surface half done by Thread H 09.T9b (body-lite-surface extension, 23/23 tests). All four 09.T9 verification dimensions satisfied: (1) Theia tests open S5 snapshots and inspect route/evidence/review chains (control-room half); (2) /body tests show alerts from real DTOs and refuse approve/reject/revise for human-required gates (defer-only contract test, lite-surface half); (3) cross-surface deep-link payload preservation verified across all 8 BodyDeepLinkContext fields; (4) one kernel-bridge subscription invariant asserted.
